summary: Complete testing of SKU Management CRUD module. Backend: 20/20 tests passed (100%). Frontend: All UI features working correctly. Integration: Lead Detail and Account Detail pages successfully fetch SKUs from master list API.
passed_tests: Backend: GET /api/master-skus returns 200 with SKUs from database
Backend: Response contains 'skus' array with required fields (id, sku_name, category, unit, is_active)
Backend: Default returns only active SKUs
Backend: include_inactive=true returns deactivated SKUs
Backend: GET /api/sku-categories returns unique categories (Jar, Bottle, Premium, Sparkling, Can, White Label)
Backend: POST /api/master-skus creates new SKU and returns 200
Backend: Created SKU persists in database (verified via GET)
Backend: Duplicate SKU name returns 400 error
Backend: PUT /api/master-skus/{id} updates SKU successfully
Backend: Updated fields persist in database
Backend: PUT with non-existent ID returns 404
Backend: DELETE /api/master-skus/{id} soft-deletes (deactivates) SKU
Backend: Soft-deleted SKU appears with include_inactive=true
Backend: Reactivate SKU via PUT with is_active=true works
Backend: DELETE with non-existent ID returns 404
Backend: SKUs come from MongoDB (not hardcoded) - new SKUs appear dynamically
Frontend: SKU Management page loads at /sku-management
Frontend: Page shows Total SKUs, Active count, Categories count, Filtered count stats
Frontend: SKUs grouped by category with colored badges
Frontend: Add New SKU button opens modal with form
Frontend: Create SKU modal has fields: SKU Name, Category dropdown, Unit, Description, Sort Order, Status toggle
Frontend: Category dropdown shows all 6 categories + Add New Category option
Frontend: Created SKU appears in list with success toast 'SKU created successfully'
Frontend: Edit button opens modal pre-filled with existing data
Frontend: Edited fields persist with success toast 'SKU updated successfully'
Frontend: Delete button shows confirmation, deactivates SKU with toast 'SKU deactivated'
Frontend: Deactivated SKU hidden from default view
Frontend: Show Inactive toggle reveals deactivated SKUs with 'Inactive' badge
Frontend: Reactivate button on inactive SKUs works with toast 'SKU reactivated'
Frontend: Search filter filters SKUs by name
Frontend: Category filter filters SKUs by category
Integration: Lead Detail page SKU dropdown shows 15 SKUs from master list API
Integration: Account Detail page SKU dropdown shows 15 SKUs from master list API
critical_code_review_comments: SKU CRUD endpoints in server.py (lines 720-898) properly implemented with MongoDB persistence
Soft delete pattern correctly used - DELETE marks is_active=False instead of removing document
Default SKUs seeded on first load via seed_default_skus() function
skusAPI in api.js correctly implements getMasterList, create, update, delete, getCategories
SKUManagement.js properly implements full CRUD UI with modals, filters, and status toggle

All data-testid attributes present for testing (create-sku-btn, sku-name-input, save-sku-btn, edit-sku-{id}, delete-sku-{id}, show-inactive-toggle, search-sku-input, filter-category-select, sku-row-{id}, reactivate-sku-{id})
LeadDetail.js and AccountDetail.js correctly fetch and display SKUs from master list API
seed_data_creation: 14 default SKUs seeded automatically on first API call. Test SKUs created during testing were cleaned up.
context_for_next_testing_agent: SKU Management CRUD fully working. Key test-ids: create-sku-btn, sku-name-input, sku-category-select, sku-unit-input, sku-description-input, sku-sort-order-input, sku-active-toggle, save-sku-btn, edit-sku-{id}, delete-sku-{id}, reactivate-sku-{id}, show-inactive-toggle, search-sku-input, filter-category-select, sku-row-{id}. Master SKUs stored in 'master_skus' MongoDB collection. SKU dropdown in Lead Detail uses proposed-sku-select-{index}, in Account Detail uses sku-select-{index}.
